mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates month-over-month, signaling robust labor demand and consumer income expansion. This drives near-term equity valuations upward as forward earnings expectations improve, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2009 recovery phase with employment stabilization
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Payroll growth acceleration does not precede positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains below 3.5% while inflation (CPI) stays below 2.5%, indicating accommodative monetary conditions. This low real rate environment compresses equity risk premiums and supports equity valuations, producing positive forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances when both conditions are met, or Sharpe ratio of the signal is < 0.3 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline by more than 10% month-over-month, signaling deteriorating demand expectations and reduced construction activity. This negative demand shock propagates to equity valuations through lower earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances despite housing decline, or the signal produces negative Sharpe ratio over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates above its 3-month moving average while unemployment remains stable below 5.5%, indicating supply-side expansion without labor market stress. This productivity-driven growth supports equity earnings, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: supply-driven expansion with labor market slack
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal produces lower Rank IC than either component alone over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth turns negative year-over-year, indicating consumer demand contraction. This demand destruction reduces forward earnings expectations across consumer-sensitive sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when RSAFS growth turns negative, or the signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points over a 3-month period while payroll employment declines month-over-month, signaling labor market deterioration. This dual deterioration triggers equity risk-off behavior, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 5.1%, PAYEMS at 133347.0 thousand
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances despite labor market weakness, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 2-month period, signaling monetary policy easing. This rate decline reduces discount rates on future corporate cash flows and signals economic weakness concerns, producing positive equity returns over 5 trading days as markets price in stimulus.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle or crisis response
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or the signal produces negative Sharpe ratio over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized while retail sales growth remains positive, indicating broad-based demand expansion. This synchronized demand growth supports equity valuations across sectors, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS year-over-year growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 11088.8 billion chained 2017 dollars, RSAFS at 343623.0 million
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion or late-cycle growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances despite synchronized demand growth, or the interaction signal produces lower Rank IC than GDPC1 alone over any 3-year window.
